title: Saag paneer
subtitle: Spinach curry with fried paneer cubes
excerpt: Smooth, spiced spinach sauce enriched with cream and dotted with fried cubes of paneer that hold their shape in the heat. The spinach is blended to a silky paste after wilting, which gives the dish its distinctive deep-green sauce. A vegetarian centrepiece of the British Indian menu.
sourceNotes: Saag paneer (palak paneer in North Indian terminology) is a Punjabi dish of spinach with paneer cheese, documented in Madhur Jaffrey's 'Indian Cookery' (1982). In the British curry-house tradition it is one of the most popular vegetarian main dishes.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the spinach sauce ahead. Fry the paneer fresh just before serving.

content.text: The paneer is fried first in a very hot pan with a little oil until the outside is golden and slightly crisp. This seals the exterior and stops the cheese breaking up in the sauce. Don't try to move the cubes until the first side has set; they will release from the pan cleanly when ready. Once added to the spinach sauce, they absorb the flavour while keeping their form.

Blending the spinach is the step that distinguishes saag paneer from saag aloo. Wilt the spinach completely, then blend with the spiced onion mixture until smooth. A stick blender in the pan works well. The resulting sauce should be dark green, smooth, and thick enough to coat a spoon.

Heat the {{ghee}} in a wide pan over high heat. Add the {{paneer}} cubes in a single layer. Cook without moving for 2 to 3 minutes until golden on the first side, then turn and repeat. Remove and set aside.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in the same pan. Add the {{onion}} and cook for 8 minutes until golden. Add the {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{chilli-green}}, {{turmeric}}, {{cumin-ground}}, and {{coriander-ground}} and cook for 2 minutes. Add the {{spinach}} in batches and wilt completely. Blend the mixture until smooth using a stick blender. Return to the heat, add the {{double-cream}} if using, season with salt, and stir in the {{garam-masala}}. Add the fried paneer cubes and stir gently to coat. Serve immediately.

items.cause: Pan not hot enough or paneer moved too soon
items.fix: Let the ghee get properly hot before adding the paneer. Don't move the cubes until they release cleanly from the pan

items.cause: Too much water from spinach not cooked off
items.fix: Cook the wilted spinach until all visible moisture has evaporated before blending. After blending, simmer the sauce to reduce if needed

items.symptom: Sauce is khaki rather than bright green
items.fix: Do not overcook the spinach. Wilt it just until soft, then blend immediately. Overcooking destroys the chlorophyll and dulls the colour

items.symptom: Paneer is rubbery after adding to sauce
items.cause: Sauce kept too hot after paneer added
items.fix: Add the paneer to the finished sauce off the heat or on very low heat. It only needs to warm through, not cook further

content.text: Saag paneer is the vegetarian main on the British curry-house menu that works as a full meal rather than just a side. The fried paneer provides substance; the spiced spinach provides depth. It sits alongside dal makhani and chana masala as one of the three dishes that made vegetarian eating at the British curry-house not merely possible but decidedly desirable. The dish arrived in British Indian restaurants in the 1970s and has been on menus ever since.
